Details: USEPA has identified coagulation/filtration as Best Avaialble Technology (BAT) for chromium control in drinking water. Removal of chromium (VI) in water by conventional processes can be very effective under appropriate treatment conditions.   One paper studied the chromium removal by reduction using ferrous sulfate, coagulation assisted by aeration, followed by medial filtration.  Removal efficiencies up to 100 percent can be achieved under optimized treatment conditions.  Reduction pH and aeration/filtration pH was found to be influential factor for chromium removal.  Mass ratio of Fe:Cr and loading rate had minimal impact on chromium (VI) removal.
